Polling, Insights, and Campaign Strategy
QuantusInsights.org | @quantusinsights on X
Methodology Summary
Sample: 1,000 registered voters | Fielded June 23-25, 2025 | Published June 26, 2025
Mode: Mixed-mode approach using opt-in online panels and SMS outreach
Voter Profile of Registered Voters Surveyed
Totals Male Female White Black Hispanic Other 18-29 30-44 45-64 65+ College Non-college
Pct 48% 52% 72% 11% 11% 6% 15% 23% 34% 28% 39% 62%
Totals Republican Democrat Independent
Pct 34% 33% 33%
Totals Urban Suburban Rural
Pct 28% 52% 20%

Q13. If the 2026 general election for Congress were held today, which party's
candidate would you be more likely to vote for in your district?
Democratic Party candidate
Republican Party candidate
Other
No opinion
Generic Congressional Ballot by Demographic
White Black Hispanic Other
Democratic candidate 34% 76% 49% 43%
Republican candidate 55% 12% 34% 38%
Other 4% 3% 5% 6%
No opinion 7% 9% 12% 13%
18-29 30-44 45-64 65+
Democratic candidate 55% 49% 42% 40%
Republican candidate 31% 39% 47% 50%
Other 4% 3% 4% 3%
No opinion 10% 9% 7% 7%
College Non-college
Democratic candidate 51% 39%
Republican candidate 38% 50%
Other 3% 4%
No opinion 8% 7%
